name: Wadi Naqab
desc: A dramatic seasonal wadi high in the Hajar Mountains below Jebel Jais — red-rock walls, terraced pools and some of the clearest mountain air and darkest skies in Ras Al Khaimah.
body:
Wadi Naqab cuts through the north-western Hajar Mountains of Ras Al Khaimah, on the flanks of Jebel Jais — the UAE's highest peak at 1,934m. Red-rock walls rise on either side of a seasonal watercourse that fills with terraced pools after the winter rains, and the trail is a well-loved scramble for hikers who want real mountains within a couple of hours of the city.

High, remote and far from the coastal glow, Naqab holds onto genuinely dark mountain skies (around Bortle 3) — on a clear, moonless night the stars come down to the ridgelines. Come in the cooler months, carry more water than you think you need, wear proper grip for the rock, and give yourself time: the light on the peaks at dusk is the reason to be here. A high-clearance vehicle reaches the trailhead; the wadi itself is on foot.
